Incident Report — Cyber/Operational Disclosure (US issuer, healthcare + banking) — 2026-06-03 21:40 ET: Everwell Therapeutics, Inc. (fictional) with processor bank Pioneer River Bank, N.A. (fictional) identified a misconfiguration that exposed a cloud folder to an external research domain for 23 minutes during an investor-relations dry run.
A OneDrive link labeled IR-Preview_Q2.xlsx and Trial_Apollo_Readout.pptx was auto-shared to jpike@hearthstone-capital.example (Mr. Jonas Pike, equity analyst, fictional) when an invite template was reused from a prior non-deal roadshow.
Content included preliminary ranges for Q2 net interest income from HSA custodial accounts and an early, not-yet-public clinical response rate (62% at n=128) for Program Apollo; this is potentially material non-public information under Regulation FD and raises Rule 10b-5 concerns regarding selective disclosure to a covered market actor.
We immediately revoked the link, preserved access logs, and are preparing broad public disclosure; trading operations are normal and a halt is not anticipated absent further developments.
The folder also contained an operational export (GLBA/HIPAA-adjacent) listing patient age bands, 3‑digit ZIPs, benefit card BINs truncated to last 4, and device models; no full names, no SSNs, and no diagnosis codes were present, and under CCPA/HIPAA/GLBA this data may be reasonably linkable only in combination.
Mr. Luis Garner (fictional) — Endpoint Administrator at Everwell Therapeutics, Inc., based in North Braxton, PA 00091 — b. 08/14/1986 (age 39), signed in from device EW‑LG‑021 (Windows 11), account lgarner@everwell-care.com, via VPN sess‑ 8 3 4 1 ‑A9, desk ext x4128, and misapplied a “external edit” policy to the IR folder.
Associated artifacts include app token D E: AD: BE: EF: 00:12 (application key string, not a device identifier) and a reset URL https://portal.everwell-care.com/reset?uid=af 4 2 - 7 0 9 &ts=1726 0 5 used to revoke the share; the audit trail shows outbound traffic to hearthstone-capital.example for 4 file previews, no downloads.
Similar weak identifiers exist elsewhere in the corpus without linkage to a person (e.g., DOB month-year 03/1979; ZIP3 606; device “iOS 17”; account ending ••7310; session code R- 1 9 0 - B), and these alone should not be treated as identifying.
Proxy and CASB logs confirm only jpike@hearthstone-capital.example accessed the link; the analyst viewed chart thumbnails but did not authenticate into any internal system, and we see no lateral movement.
We did not collect SSNs or device MAC addresses in this workflow; “MAC” in our tickets refers to mandatory access control, not media access control; product names “Endpoint Shield 4.2” and “Orion EHR Bridge” appear in logs as configuration labels only.
Ms. Priya Dalton (fictional), Chief Risk Officer, approved initial notification under our incident playbook; questions may be sent to compliance@everwell-care.com or 1‑833‑555‑0190 (fictional).
To address potential Reg FD exposure, IR will post a press release with the same ranges and trial context and furnish a contemporaneous current report under federal securities law by 08:30 ET prior to market open; no forward-looking guidance beyond what will be broadly disclosed is authorized.
Customer communications under GLBA and business-associate obligations will note that only age bands, ZIP3, device model, and truncated payment card BINs were in scope; we have initiated mailing suppression to avoid duplicate notices across households.
project note (non‑determinative): move “q2-readout” to a separate channel after redteam re-check; this line is generic workflow guidance and contains no consumer or patient data.
Public-source references (e.g., prior conference posters and last quarter’s earnings deck) were cited in the draft slides for benchmarking; these citations do not constitute new MNPI and will remain intact after redaction.
